violations.explanation: SW HLR and HW HWR both reference interface 'FPU_CSR_BLOCK' but consistency is not asserted (no CONSISTENT_WITH edge). Cross-domain coherence required by ARP4754A §5.

CROSS-TIMING-1.title: Timing budget allocations consistent across SW and HW

violations.explanation: System requirement 'UAV-SYS-40' has timing budget 2000000 ns, but allocations to SW+HW sum to 2500000 ns. Budget overrun in cross-domain allocation.

CROSS-SAFETY-1.title: Safety hazards propagated to both SW and HW requirements

violations.explanation: Safety hazard 'UAV-HAZ-2' (severity: hazardous) requires both SW and HW mitigation but lacks one or both. ARP4761 + DO-178C/DO-254 cross-domain mitigation.

raw_data.hazard_text: Motor runaway: actuator command path emits a saturated PWM value after a stale rate setpoint.

seeded_violations.description: UAV-SYS-40 (failsafe) has a 2,000,000 ns budget but its children UAV-HLR-40 (1,500,000) + UAV-HWR-40 (1,000,000) sum to 2,500,000 ns — a 500,000 ns overrun.
seeded_violations.why: Single-domain tools see each child fitting its own share; only a cross-domain sum detects the system-budget overrun.

seeded_violations.description: UAV-HLR-60 and UAV-HWR-60 both MENTIONS the FPU_CSR_BLOCK interface, but no CONSISTENT_WITH edge is asserted between them.
seeded_violations.why: Mismatched HW/SW interface specs are a classic integration defect that slips through single-domain verification.

seeded_violations.description: UAV-HAZ-2 (hazardous, dual-domain required) is mitigated only on the HW side (UAV-HWR-50); no HLR MITIGATES edge exists.
seeded_violations.why: Catastrophic/hazardous hazards needing defence-in-depth require mitigation on BOTH sides; single-domain coverage is insufficient.

_provenance.nature: DERIVED, ILLUSTRATIVE dataset authored for this study — NOT requirements from a real certified UAV program and NOT certification evidence. This supersedes the hand-authored synthetic cross-domain seed in scripts/load_neo4j.py.
sw_side_derived_from: PX4 architecture/controller concept (docs.px4.io/main/en/concept/architecture.html): IMU 1 kHz sample, 250 Hz publish; cascaded position/attitude/rate controllers
PX4 control-loop cadence (discuss.px4.io/t/control-loop-update-frequency/14043): rate loop ~250 Hz, position loop ~100 Hz
ArduPilot aggressive rate-loop tuning (ardupilot.org/copter/docs/high-loop-rate-tuning.html): main loop 400 Hz / 2.5 ms; fast-rate thread up to 4 kHz
ArduPilot failsafe documentation (ardupilot.org/copter/docs/failsafe-landing-page.html): bounded deterministic link-loss/battery/EKF failsafes
_provenance.hw_side_anchored_in: CVA6 Requirements Specification v1.0.1 (OpenHW Group/Thales) via specguard.data.cva6_requirements; HWR reqs reference real CVA6 IDs IRQ-10, MEM-10, MEM-20, L1W-10, FET-10, ISA-60
_provenance.authored_for_this_study: 'shall' requirement wording, HLR/HWR decomposition, timing-budget arithmetic, DAL assignments, mini-FHA hazards and severities, and all deliberate seeded violations
